public_reputation: Known around Fairhaven through day-to-day work as local cleric.
appearance: Serin Sunweave carries the marks of local cleric in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Balances comfort, warning, and ritual order, reading disrespect or fear before others admit it aloud. In Fairhaven, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of local cleric in and around Fairhaven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Fairhaven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Serin Sunweave can draw the party into a locality problem centered on local cleric duties in Fairhaven.
Serin Sunweave becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with glyph corruption, celestial creatures at Watcher’s Perch, suspicious travelers in taverns around Fairhaven.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Serin Sunweave is immediately readable as a local cleric tied to Fairhaven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Fairhaven: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Serin Sunweave presents as someone shaped by the duties of a local cleric and the daily pressures of Fairhaven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a local cleric, and proof that strangers will not make Fairhaven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Serin Sunweave refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a local cleric too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can explain ritual expectations, mediate tension, and reduce social or spiritual fallout.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Serin Sunweave adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Serin Sunweave always knows more about Fairhaven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a local cleric in Fairhaven.
social_texture: Serin Sunweave speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Fairhaven, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Serin touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Serin Sunweave when the party needs a socially grounded face for Fairhaven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
